task_completion.description: The Graphite dashboard interface is open and being edited.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite web UI is visible with the Dashboard view active, showing a workspace for adding graphs.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Traffic Share Percentage' is created using the asPercent() function on the correct metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph with the title 'Traffic Share Percentage' is visible. In its configuration, metric tree, or legend, the metric 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_*' and the 'asPercent()' function should be visible.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Cumulative LB Requests' is created using the integral() function on the correct metric and colored blue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph with the title 'Cumulative LB Requests' is visible, the plotted line is blue, and its configuration or legend shows the 'integral()' and 'color()' functions applied to 'servers.load_balancer.requests.count'.

task_completion.description: A graph titled 'Network Ingress Volume' is created using the cactiStyle() function on the correct metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph with the title 'Network Ingress Volume' is visible, and its configuration or legend shows the 'cactiStyle()' function applied to 'servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in'.

task_completion.description: The dashboard is saved with the name 'Traffic Distribution Report'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard title at the top of the screen or in the save dialog reads 'Traffic Distribution Report', indicating the dashboard has been properly named and saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Graphite web interface to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the Graphite web UI (e.g., clicking through the metric tree, using the composer, or dashboard UI) rather than a script or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly writing a JSON file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) open with JSON dashboard definitions being manually typed out.

integrity.description: The graphs are genuinely rendered by the Graphite application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should look like standard Graphite rendered images with axes, legends, and data lines, not mocked or pasted images.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is 'Traffic Distribution Report'. The three required graph titles are 'Traffic Share Percentage', 'Cumulative LB Requests', and 'Network Ingress Volume'. The required functions are asPercent, integral (with blue color), and cactiStyle applied to their respective metrics.
